verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: A modern reader must sometimes be struck with finding in St. Chrysostom a kind of criticism, which we are apt to think belongs only to later times.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: His main object, however, is moral, and he searches out with diligence both the meaning and the applications of particular passages, usually concluding with an eloquent exhortation to some special virtue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Some of the most remarkable of these exhortations are on the subject of Alms-giving, which he seems to have pressed with some success at last.

verses.text: It is perhaps worth while to recollect the nearly contemporaneous prevalence of Manichæism in the West, as it appears in the early history of St. Augustine.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: In Hom. lxxxvi. there are some remarks on the device of Satan by which evil is introduced by little and little, which are worthy of consideration as applicable to the growth of erroneous doctrine and practice within the Church.
